channel.title: Ad News Feed
channel.description: 広告プラットフォーム最新情報の自動収集フィード

item.title: [Google Ads] Have you tried AI Max yet?
item.description:
Everyone’s talking about AI Max for Google Ads…
Here’s the honest truth from someone who’s actually tested it.
No meaningful results (yet) And the expectation of seeing any real impact? We’re probably talking 6 months - 2 two years away minimum. (like when PMAX was launched in 2022)
Even for eco

item.title: [Google Ads] Double Your CTR! Every Google Ads Asset Explained [Inside Google Ads Episode 118]
item.description: If you're still running search ads with basic headlines and descriptions, you are firmly average. And in 2026, average just doesn't cut it anymore! In episode 118 of the Inside Google Ads podcast, Google Ads Coach Jyll Saskin Gales walks you through all 15 types of Google Ads assets (formerly known

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Ads Broad Match: The Competitive Advantage Explained #shorts
item.description: Unlock the power of Google Ads! Discover why broad match, with a higher budget, can offer a long-term competitive edge despite initial spend. Learn how exact match and its synonyms are continuously expanding. #GoogleAds #DigitalMarketing #SEO #PPC #AdvertisingTips

item.title: [Google Ads] New: Task Assistant in Google Analytics – Check Your Setup
item.description: Learn how to use Google Analytics' Task Assistant to make sure your property is set up correctly and you're not missing any important features or integrations. Task Assistant is a built-in checklist that shows you exactly what needs to be configured in your Google Analytics property, and it automati

item.title: [Meta Ads] Bringing Prepaid Mobile Recharges to WhatsApp Users in India

I run Heath Media, a marketing agency that helps businesses sca

item.title: [Google Ads] How to Scale Google Ads .... (profitably)
item.description:
Throwing more budget at your Google Ads campaigns and watching your CPCs blow out? Or hitting a ceiling where extra spend just disappears with no extra conversions to show for it?
In this video I'm pulling back the curtain on the exact 4-step framework we use inside Define Digital Academy to scale

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Ads: Broad Match vs. Phrase Match Strategy #shorts
item.description: Understand keyword match types for better ad campaign performance. Learn how broad match can be a competitive advantage with high budgets and strong conversion data. #KeywordStrategy #PPC #DigitalMarketing #SEOtips

item.title: [Meta Ads] Are You Setting Up Client Access the Wrong Way?
item.description: Today's question is about the recommended structure and access levels between a client and agency when onboarding. Setting this up incorrectly can lead to serious problems, including getting accounts banned. Jon explains why the client should own all of their own assets and add your agency as a part

item.title: [Google Ads] Where to Find the Google Ads Tag (and Install It with GTM)
item.description: Learn how to find the Google Ads Tag and install it on your website using Google Tag Manager. The Google Ads Tag was previously found under Audience Manager, but it has moved, in this tutorial I'll show you exactly where to find it now, how to copy your tag ID, and how to add it to your website usin

item.title: [Meta Ads] Introducing Ads CLI
item.description: Today we're introducing the Meta ads CLI, a command-line tool for developers to manage Meta ad campaigns. Developers and AI agents working with the Meta Marketing API can now create, edit, and analyze campaigns directly from the command line, without writing custom code.

item.title: [Google Ads] GA4の探索レポートで広告運用者が見るべき6つの数値 設定手順つきで解説
item.description:
Google アナリティクス 4 プロパティ（以下、GA4）には、全体の数値や主要なデータを簡単に確認できる「標準レポート」の他に、カスタムレポートを作成し、特定のデータを深く分析できる「探索レポート…

The post GA4の探索レポートで広告運用者が見るべき6つの数値 設定手順つきで解説 first appeared on アナグラム株式会社.

item.title: [General] YouTube広告でチャンネル登録者を増やすには？設定手順と運用のコツを事例つきで解説
item.description:
電通が発表した「2025年 日本の広告費」によると、ビデオ（動画）広告市場は前年比121.8％の1兆275億円に達し、推定開始以来初めて1兆円を突破しました。動画広告は今やインターネット広告媒体費の30％超を占め、企業の …

"YouTube広告でチャンネル登録者を増やすには？設定手順と運用

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Ads App Labs Beta: What App Advertisers Need to Know About Experimental Features in the App Advertising Hub
item.description: Google has begun testing App Labs inside the app advertising hub in Google Ads, and the early signal is important even though the public information is still limited. What appears to be confirmed so far is straightforward: selected app advertisers may see a beta area that gives them access to lim

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Security Agent for Account Hygiene: What Google Ads Advertisers Need to Know About Access Control, Passkeys, and Account Risk
item.description: Google’s Security Agent for account hygiene is one of the more important recent changes in Google Ads account governance because it shifts security from occasional manual review to ongoing monitoring. For advertisers, agencies, in-house marketing teams, and multi-account managers, that change mat

item.title: [Google Ads] The End of Manual Bidding Levers: How to Win With Smart Bidding, Performance Max, and Value-Based PPC
item.description: For most of paid media’s history, advertisers won by managing dozens of small controls better than the next account. They set keyword bids by hand. They split campaigns by match type. They adjusted bids by device, geography, hour, audience, and network. They watched search terms closely, pushed s

item.title: [General] The Rise of AI Purchasing Agents: 12 Practical Ways to Optimize for Non-Human Buyers
item.description: If your team still assumes every buyer journey begins with a human reading your homepage, comparing a few pages, and filling out a form, that assumption is aging fast. A growing share of discovery, comparison, and shortlisting is now mediated by AI systems. In consumer contexts, that may look lik

item.title: [General] Brand Trust Is Now a Performance Metric, Not Just a Brand Metric: How to Measure It, Prove ROI, and Turn Trust Into Growth
item.description: There was a time when brand trust lived mostly in the “soft metrics” bucket. It sat next to awareness, recall, favorability, and share of voice. Important, yes. Commercially useful, probably. But often treated as something separate from the hard numbers that executives actually used to run the bu

item.title: [General] Answer Engine Optimization in 2026: A Practical Playbook for Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Claude
item.description: Search is no longer just a list of blue links. In 2026, many users begin with an AI-generated answer, a synthesized overview, a voice response, or a conversational interface that pulls from multiple sources at once. That shift has changed how brands earn visibility. Traditional SEO still matters,

item.title: [General] Google Expands AI Search Mode to YouTube: What Ask YouTube Means for Search, Discovery, Creators, and Brands
item.description: Google is extending its AI-driven search experience into YouTube, and that matters for more than product news. item.title: [General] Snapchat Sponsored AI Chatbots : What AI Sponsored Snaps Mean for Brands, Ads, and Users

item.title: [General] Should you use AI Max?
item.description:
My honest take on AI Max for Search campaigns (recorded April 2026):
I think it has potential. But right now, I’m not prepared to put my name behind it. And here’s the thing most people aren’t saying out loud: the vast majority of businesses don’t need AI Max yet.
You can scale profitably with thi

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Data Studio is Back!?!
item.description:
Google has announced that Looker Studio is now Data Studio. The change includes a new logo, updated interface and new conversational analysis feature. Learn more about the new Google Data Studio in this video.
Join our courses: https://www.lovesdata.com/courses/ Subscribe to our YouTube channel: ht

That

item.title: [General] Shoppable CTV in 2026: Why E-commerce Brands Need Streaming Ads That Convert

Connected TV has moved from a mostly upper-funnel channel into a measurable commerce environment, and shoppable CTV is the clear

item.title: [General] Agentic AI vs. Traditional Automation: The Q3 Planning Guide for Agency Owners on ROI, Cost, and Use Cases

Which services can scale without adding headcount at the same pace as revenue? Which workflows still depend too heavily on senior people? And wh

item.title: [General] Paramount and Wonder Works Launch SpongeBob Tower Defense 2.0 on Roblox: Features, Stats, Roadmap, and What the Update Means
item.description: When a licensed game gets a major update, the usual story is straightforward: new content arrives, the publisher posts an announcement, players log in to see what changed, and the news cycle moves on. SpongeBob Tower Defense 2.0 on Roblox is not that kind of update. This relaunch matters because

item.title: [General] Diet Coke’s ‘Canny Pack’ : How the Devil Wears Prada 2 Campaign Turns a Single Can Into a Fashion Accessory

It does not look like a standard movie tie-in with a logo attached to a poster, a few paid social posts, and a short-lived prom

item.title: [General] WPP Open Integrates Google Earth AI: What Marketers Should Know About Media Planning, Audience Intelligence, and a 77% Performance Lift
item.description: Marketing teams have spent years getting better at reading digital signals. What they have not always seen clearly is the physical context around those actions: the weather on the day dema

item.description: Smoothie King did not present its latest move as a cosmetic rebrand. It presented it as a brand evolution, and that distinction matters. item.title: [Google Ads] How Voice Search Is Changing Google Ads Search Term Reports in 2026: 12 Practical Fixes to Cut Waste and Improve Intent Matching
item.description: If you manage Google Ads closely, you can see the difference immediately in the search term report. The old pattern was compact and predictable. People typed short, compressed phrases like “emergency plumber,” “crm software pricing,” or “best running shoes.” Today, more queries arrive in the form

item.title: [LinkedIn Ads] LinkedIn Advertising: 21 Advanced Tactics to Improve Lead Quality, Lower CPL, and Scale B2B Pipeline

item.title: [General] Ubersuggest Review and Guide (Updated April 2026): Features, Pricing, Accuracy, Pros, Cons, and Best Uses

item.description: Connected TV advertising has moved from an experimental media line to a standard part of modern video planning. For many brands, that shift did not happen because CTV was new. It happened because audience behavior changed first. People now watch a large share of TV through streaming environments

item.description: Client retention in white label SEO is rarely lost in one dramatic moment. Most accounts do not disappear because of a single bad month, one missed keyword target, or one awkward meeting. They leave because several small problems go unaddressed at the same time: expectations start to drift, commu

The campaign, titled “Trust What’s Inside,”

item.title: [General] White Label SEO for Franchises: A Practical Guide to Location Page Architecture, Brand Consistency, and Scalable Citation Management
item.description: Franchise SEO looks straightforward from the outside. A brand has one name, one broad service set, and multiple locations. Instead, it stays rooted in a very familiar experience: too many opinions, too much noise, and not enough clarity. In “Your Health. In Your Hands.”, Apple frames th

Titled The Scene and written by and starring Rachel Sennott, the pre-fall 2026 c

item.title: [General] OpenAI GPT-5.5 Explained: Benchmarks, Pricing, API Access, Safety, and GPT-5.5 vs GPT-5.4
item.description: OpenAI’s GPT-5.5 is not just another model update with a slightly higher benchmark score. It is a clearer statement of how OpenAI wants AI systems to be used in real work: less as one-turn chatbots and more as systems that can carry a task across multiple steps, multiple tools, and multiple forms

item.title: [General] Snapchat Place Loyalty on Snap Map: How Top Visitor Badges Work, What They Mean, and Why This Update Matters
item.description: Snapchat has added a new layer to Snap Map that turns repeat visits into a visible in-app status signal. The feature, called Place Loyalty, assigns Top Visitor badges to users who visit certain places often enough to rank near the top of that location’s visitors. On the surface, that sounds simpl

item.title: [General] Meta Expands Instagram Management APIs: What the New Instagram API Updates Mean for Brands, Creators, and Developers
item.description: Meta has expanded Instagram management APIs in ways that matter for three groups immediately: brands running partnership campaigns, creators who publish sponsored content through third-party tools, and developers building publishing, analytics, moderation, and reporting workflows around Instagram

item.title: [General] YouTube Reduces Push Notifications for Inactive Subscribers: What Creators Need to Know About Reach, Engagement, and Channel Growth
item.description: If your YouTube strategy has ever depended on subscribers clicking the bell, this update matters. YouTube is reducing mobile push notifications for certain subscribers who selected “All” notifications but have not watched or engaged with a channel for about a month. Those viewers are not being re

item.title: [General] Meta’s Instants App : How Instagram’s Standalone Disappearing-Photo Test Works, Where It’s Available, and Why It Matters
item.description: Meta is testing a new standalone app tied to Instagram called Instants, and the concept is easy to grasp: open straight to the camera, take a quick photo, send it to friends, and let it disappear after it is viewed or after a short window passes. On the surface, that sounds familiar because it is

item.title: [General] Meta Now Lets Parents See What Teens Ask Meta AI: How the New Insights Tool Works, What Parents Can See, and What It Means
item.description: Meta has added a new layer to its parental supervision system: parents who supervise Teen Accounts can now see the topics their teens have asked Meta AI about over the previous seven days. On the surface, that sounds simple. In practice, it marks an important shift in how one of the world’s large

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Adds Sponsored Stores to the Shopping Tab: What Retailers Need to Know Now
item.description: Google is showing a new store-level sponsored format inside the Shopping tab, and that matters because it changes the unit of visibility. For years, most advertisers have treated Google Shopping as a product-level environment. A shopper searches, Google matches the query to Merchant Center data,

item.title: [General] Boost Your Google Rankings with Review Velocity! #shorts
item.description: Focus on review velocity: how many new 5-star reviews you get monthly. Google favors businesses with high review counts, especially from local guides who add photos. This engagement drives higher rankings. #ReviewVelocity #GoogleMyBusiness #LocalSEO #BusinessGrowth

item.title: [Meta Ads] Meta Partners With AWS on Graviton Chips to Power Agentic AI
[General] KitKat Canada’s “KatKit” Mix-Up : How the Courage Campaign Launched Mix-Ups With a Simple Brand Twist
item.description: If you’ve seen a KitKat logo turned into “KatKit” and wondered whether it was a printing error, a one-off stunt, or part of something bigger, the short answer is simple: it was a deliberate campaign by KitKat Canada to introduce a new product called Mix-Ups. The execution was intentionally small

item.title: [General] Paramount+ App Redesign 2026: What the New Short-Form Video Feed Means for Streaming, Discovery, and Engagement
item.description: For years, streaming apps were built around a simple assumption: people opened them when they were ready to sit down and watch something for a meaningful stretch of time. The interface reflected that behavior. Large title art, rows of shows, personalized carousels, and a home screen designed to p

item.title: [General] Mattel’s 400-Drone He-Man Billboards at Coachella: What Happened, Why They Worked, and What Brands Can Learn
item.description: When people talk about outdoor advertising, they usually mean roadside placements, transit media, stadium signage, or large-format digital screens. Mattel’s Masters of the Universe activation in the California desert pushed that definition further. Instead of buying attention only on the side of

item.title: [General] Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra and The Devil Wears Prada 2: What the Collaboration Means for Fashion, Film, AI Features, and Premium Smartphone Marketing
item.description: Samsung has found a clean way to place the Galaxy S26 Ultra inside a cultural conversation that already knows how to command attention. Instead of treating the phone launch as a standalone hardware story, the company tied it to The Devil Wears Prada 2, a sequel that arrives with built-in recognit

item.title: [General] Universal Pictures’ Michael Architectural DOOH Campaign – How Milan and Berlin Turned Landmark Buildings Into Movie Media
item.description: When Universal Pictures International launched the outdoor campaign for Michael, it did not use digital billboards in the usual sense. Instead, it used architecture itself as the medium. Two prominent European locations, Rinascente in Milan and Uber Platz in Berlin, were transformed into large-sc

item.title: [General] L’Oréal Paris and Ulta Beauty’s “Mother & ___” Campaign With Eva Longoria: What the 2026 Mother’s Day Beauty Retail Push Means
item.description: Mother’s Day campaigns often follow a familiar pattern. Brands focus on gift giving, sentiment, and broad emotional language that treats motherhood as a single, all-defining identity. L’Oréal Paris and Ulta Beauty took a different approach in 2026. Their “Mother & ___” campaign, led by Eva Lo

item.title: [General] Gwyneth Paltrow’s Goop Kitchen Comes to New York: Menu, Locations, Delivery Strategy, and What NYC Diners Can Expect
item.description: When Gwyneth Paltrow’s Goop Kitchen announced its move into New York, the news landed at the intersection of several trends that have been building for years: the normalization of premium takeout, the rise of ingredient-conscious dining, the steady expansion of delivery-first restaurant models, a

item.title: [General] Espolòn Tequila and Ken Jeong’s Short King Week Campaign
item.description: When a spirits brand tries to join a cultural moment, the risk is usually obvious: the campaign can feel borrowed, rushed, or detached from the product itself. That is what makes Espolòn Tequila’s Short King Week campaign with Ken Jeong more interesting than the average celebrity-backed drinks pr

item.title: [General] OpenAI Adds CPC Ads to ChatGPT
item.description: OpenAI has begun testing cost-per-click advertising in ChatGPT, and that change matters far beyond media buying mechanics. It signals that ChatGPT is no longer being treated only as a product people use for answers, research, and productivity. It is also being developed into an advertising enviro

item.title: [General] Google Chrome’s New Tab Page Is Becoming an AI Search Hub: Deep Search, Create Images, Tab Context, and What Changes for Users
item.description: For years, the Chrome new tab page was one of the simplest surfaces in consumer software. Open a tab, see a logo, type a query, move on. That simplicity is starting to change. What appeared to be a straightforward search box is increasingly being used to introduce more advanced Google search beha

item.title: [Meta Ads] A few weeks ago someone called me out about my creative roadmaps living in G sheets...
item.description:
I was offended.
However... she was RIGHT.
My friends at Replit saw this going down and offered their new Agent 4 to help.
But even though I had never vibe coded anything IN MY LIFE...
Within minutes, I had a working agency dashboard that tracks tests across all my clients.
ngl, it made me feel
